set.title: Docs, wiki publishing & dogfood discipline

Rewrite README.md — what/why: WikiTicket SDD ("wicked ticket"), fishbowled AI development, spec-driven (plans are the spec, tickets are the WIP), multi-team, system-agnostic edges (GitHub/GitLab/ADO/Jira/Confluence — they pick), epics/stories/tasks/bugs/subtasks, event-log history, generated roadmap/status. Keep quick-start + plugin sections, tightened.
Comprehensive user guide under docs/user_guide/ (excluded from plugin by construction; add packaging test)
Write docs/user_guide/user-guide.md — concepts (event log, fold, visible WIP), core workflows (plan→capture→work→close→sync, unplanned work, PR flow incl. roadmap merge recovery)
Write docs/user_guide/cli-reference.md — every worklog subcommand with examples, hooks, invariants
Write docs/user_guide/plugin-guide.md — plugin vs repo install levels, /worklog:* commands, skills, version/doctor, harness notes (Claude Code + Grok build now; OpenCode/Codex ports on roadmap)
worklog roadmap-snapshot [--name] subcommand — copy docs/roadmap.md to docs/roadmap/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<name>.md, frozen (refuse overwrite), + tests; re-copy worklog to plugin/scripts (canon sync)

wiki-publish skill (repo + plugin canonical copy) — system-vague: read .work/config.yml wiki block, publish named files using available tooling for that system (gh/git for GitHub wiki, MCP/CLI for Confluence/ADO/GitLab), research missing tooling, maintain .work/published.json ledger {key: {url, rev, source_hash}}, skip unchanged hashes, surface one-time init steps (e.g. GitHub wiki first page) to the human
Dogfood policy in CLAUDE.md — every request is broken into worklog items via work-track BEFORE work starts; mirror line in work-track skill
Enforcement hooks for the dogfood policy (plugin/hooks + repo mirror, silent outside worklog repos)
UserPromptSubmit hook — inject a one-line reminder: requests that produce work get worklog items first (work-track), keep statuses moving

Stop hook — if the working tree has non-.work changes but .work/todo.jsonl is unchanged vs HEAD, block once with "record the work items or explain" (honor stop_hook_active from stdin JSON to prevent loops)

SessionStart hook — doctor-lite on session open: verify CLAUDE.md carries the worklog policy block (marker or heading), core.hooksPath=hooks, and installed: version vs plugin version; emit additionalContext naming what's missing and pointing at /worklog:init or /worklog:doctor; silent when repo has no bin/worklog

Seed roadmap: future epics via worklog add (ticket-sync skill-based w/ GitHub Issues first P1; spec v1.4 skill-based edges P2; wiki breadth Confluence/ADO/GitLab P2; compaction+nightly CI P2; status reports + plan-next P2; OpenCode/Codex ports P2; multi-tracker simultaneous P3)

Publish wiki: Home, User-Guide, CLI-Reference, Plugin-Guide, Roadmap (current), dated snapshot page; record in .work/published.json; add .work/wiki-checkout/ to .gitignore
Plugin installer/uninstaller at repo root — install-plugin.sh (claude plugin marketplace add + install, graceful message if claude CLI absent), uninstall-plugin.sh (reverse)

set.body: Reorganize the project wiki so anyone — a new developer, a PM, an auditor — can find the right page and know whether it is current or historical. Adds a formal content model, stable page identities, truth-state banners, generated navigation and indexes, and an evidence chain from plans to releases. The full design lives in the plan doc; work proceeds in phases, foundations first.

Define one frontmatter schema that every doc type validates against, with per-type required fields, plus a small validator. Today four different frontmatter styles coexist and the roadmap has none, so tooling can't reliably read doc metadata.

Add two read-only commands: one prints the stable identity key for any doc, the other walks docs/ and produces a machine-readable inventory of every document (type, state, relationships). Also writes the migration record explaining the metadata rollout. No behavior changes yet.

Backfill every doc with its stable identity key and a current-vs-historical state. Frozen docs (plans, snapshots, old reports) are never edited — their metadata goes in sidecar files; only sanctioned living docs get in-place frontmatter. After this, every page can say what it is and whether it is current.
CI checks that every doc has an identity key, no two docs share one, and frontmatter matches the schema. Warn-only for one release so the team can adjust, then failures block merges.

Generate the reader-facing wiki pages: a question-driven Home, a sidebar grouped into Current Truth vs History, and a banner on every page saying whether it is current, a snapshot, or superseded. Readers stop guessing from file names.

The wiki publisher reads a generated manifest that says exactly which pages belong on the wiki and how each renders, replacing the implicit publish list. The hand-maintained Home page is retired in favor of the generated one.

Generate index pages so common questions have one landing spot: all decisions (ADRs and plans with supersede chains), all releases with what shipped in each, and an archive of status reports. Regenerated automatically at release and plan-capture.

Build the traceability graph linking plans to work items to tickets, code changes, tests, and releases — browsable as a wiki page and validated by a checker that reports released work missing its evidence links. Suggested links are proposed for human confirmation, never auto-written.
Turn the remaining warnings into hard CI failures, add sidebar/navigation rendering for GitLab, Azure DevOps, and Confluence wikis, and add a search command over the doc inventory.

Synced tickets showed only an internal id instead of a human-readable description, so a PM or new developer opening an issue could not tell what it was about. Fix: a description field on work items, wired through the CLI, plan capture, and both authoring skills, plus descriptions backfilled onto all open tickets.

Remove the accidentally-committed inputs/ directory from the repository's git history (it appears in two old commits) and delete the local copies. Requires a coordinated history rewrite on main, so it is scheduled rather than done ad hoc.

Nightly log compaction has failed for three nights, so old events are not being archived. Cause: a work item id was mistyped once, creating a ghost item with no creation record; when the compactor snapshots it, it fills in default fields the original never had, notices the result differs, and aborts to be safe. Fix: the compactor must write the item exactly as folded — defaults belong at creation time, not snapshot time — plus a regression test covering a closed ghost item.
set.title: Compaction aborts on closed orphan item — snapshot must not inject taxonomy defaults

set.body: Pages published to the GitHub wiki show the metadata block from the top of the source file as ugly raw text, because GitHub's wiki renderer does not understand it. Fix: the publish step strips that block before writing the wiki copy; repository files keep their metadata untouched. Already-published pages get one cleanup re-publish.
set.title: GitHub Wiki shows raw YAML frontmatter on published pages — strip at publish time

set.body: Backfill every doc with its stable identity key and a current-vs-historical state. Frozen docs (plans, snapshots, old reports) are never edited — their metadata goes in sidecar files; only sanctioned living docs get in-place frontmatter. After this, every page can say what it is and whether it is current. Review notes: decide the sidecar file layout up front (identity keys contain slashes, so sidecars need nested folders or encoded filenames), and item sidecars hold only extra links and provenance — item state always comes from the work log, never the sidecar.

Generate the reader-facing wiki pages: a question-driven Home, a sidebar grouped into Current Truth vs History, and a banner on every page saying whether it is current, a snapshot, or superseded. Readers stop guessing from file names. Review caveat: pages published before this work will not pick up banners automatically because publishing skips unchanged sources — plan either a one-time re-publish tracked by a separate render hash, or scope banners to generated pages until a migration pass runs.

set.resolution: fixed in PR #103 — fold snapshot branch no longer fabricates taxonomy defaults

set.body: Adds a pr-description skill to the plugin so substantial PRs read as durable design records: product context, exact problem, runtime caller, file-by-file changes, enforced sequence, honest testing, deliberate out-of-scope, and a ticket glossary.
set.title: PR-description skill: 10-section high-context PR body spec (big picture, runtime context, scope, flow, testing honesty, out-of-scope, ticket glossary)

set.body: Issues become durable intent records, not disposable titles: every synced ticket carries summary, big-picture context, motivation, done-criteria, and a traceability section linking the frozen plan page, epic, ADRs, and spec sections the item implements.
set.title: issue-description skill + rich ticket bodies in ticket-sync (summary/context/outcome/scope/acceptance/traceability from the item graph)

set.body: Extend the item model beyond the fixed core (id/title/status/level/kind/milestone/priority) with a configurable optional-field set, so lightweight and heavyweight processes share one log. Config block (work_item_fields in .work/config.yml) enables fields per team; every enabled field carries a description so humans AND agents use it correctly; disabled fields never appear in prompts, forms, or validation. Recommended default-on: priority, estimate, risk, owner, milestone, acceptance_criteria, labels. Default-off: value, confidence, due_date, severity, time tracking. Dependencies stay typed (blocked_by/blocks, relates_to) and feed the Traceability Index. Design principles: small stable core; explicit documented options; conservative defaults; process-agnostic. Full spec discussed 2026-07-22; re-derive the detailed field table when this is planned.
set.title: Configurable work-item field model: optional fields (estimate, risk, effort, value, confidence, owner, due_date, acceptance_criteria, blocked_by/blocks) behind work_item_fields config

set.body: The IA gates added to pre-commit import new modules during the hook, creating .pyc files after staging. A branch then commits them; the next merge into a clone whose hook regenerated the same files untracked aborts with would-be-overwritten. Fix: hooks run python with PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 so a hook can never dirty the worktree.
set.title: pre-commit hook pollutes worktree with bin/__pycache__ after git add — merge aborts on tracked-vs-untracked pyc collision
Repair frozen-plan drift: restore grok-compat-and-mermaid-viz to published version, move background-subagent rule to amendment doc
Add schema drift test: schema/doc.schema.json must stay equivalent to bin/ia.py validator constants
Schema boundary: split doc.schema.json (documents) from entity schema (items, releases, code-changes) — defer until a second graph entity needs validation
